behavior_summary: U8P_WBENCH implements a simple interactive object: when the player 'looks' at the bench the object emits a short description ('bench') and the U8P_WBENCH::look process then suspends.

The helper process U8P_WBENCH::gotHit reacts to a hit/damage event: if its first parameter is zero/false it changes the item's visual frame to 0x01 (likely to show a damaged/altered appearance) via Item::setFrame(0x01, this).
notable_control_flow: U8P_WBENCH::look is a short process that shows a single bark and then suspends, i.e., it is an inspect/description handler with no branching or external calls.
U8P_WBENCH::gotHit contains a conditional guard testing its first parameter; when that guard is satisfied (param1 == 0) it calls Item::setFrame to update the item's sprite/frame.
open_questions: What exact runtime events invoke U8P_WBENCH::gotHit (collision, weapon hit, scripted damage)?
What are the semantic meanings of U8P_WBENCH::gotHit's two parameters (param1 and param2) across the codebase — are they damage source/type, hit magnitude, or boolean flags?
Is frame 0x01 a 'damaged' state for this item, and are there other frames restored elsewhere?

main_function.name: U8P_WBENCH::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the player's short description when examining the bench (emit 'bench' and suspend/terminate the U8P_WBENCH::look process).
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player inspects/examines the bench (U8P_WBENCH::look/examine action / U8P_WBENCH::look process).

function_map.name: U8P_WBENCH::look
function_map.role: object inspect handler that presents the short description to the player

function_map.name: U8P_WBENCH::gotHit
function_map.role: damage/hit reaction handler that conditionally updates the item's display frame

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: engine/item API used to change the item's sprite/frame

external_calls.purpose: Change the item's display frame to index 0x01 (visual state change) when the U8P_WBENCH::gotHit conditional is met.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_WBENCH implements a simple interactive object: when the player 'looks' at the bench the object emits a short description ('bench') and the U8P_WBENCH::look process then suspends.
